title: JWT Attacks
summary: Your session is a JWT carrying your role. Five stages verify it a little better each time but stay forgeable — no verification, alg:none, weak secret, kid key-injection, RS256/HS256 confusion — until a signature check with a strong server-held key and a pinned algorithm.
goal: You hold a JWT whose claims say role:user. Tamper or forge the token so the server treats you as role:admin and unlocks the admin area.
goalSecure: Try the earlier forgeries (tampered claims, alg:none, weak-secret signing) — all should be rejected, while your genuine token still signs you in.
rootCause: The server derives trust from a JWT without properly verifying its signature against a strong secret and a pinned algorithm, so an attacker can forge or alter the token's claims.
lessons: A JWT is only trustworthy after its signature is verified — decoding the payload proves nothing.
Reject alg:none and pin the expected algorithm; never let the token choose how it's verified.
Sign with a strong, high-entropy secret (or asymmetric keys) — weak secrets are brute-forced offline.
Never resolve the verification key from attacker-controlled headers (kid): path traversal points it at a key you control, like the empty /dev/null.
Pin the algorithm to the key type — verifying an HS256 token with an RSA public key turns that public value into a usable HMAC secret (algorithm confusion).
Treat every claim as untrusted until verification passes, and verify on every request.

stages.title: Signature never verified
stages.defense: Reads the token's claims without verifying the signature.

stages.title: Accepts alg=none
stages.defense: Verifies HS256 — but also accepts alg:none.

stages.title: Weak signing secret
stages.defense: Verifies HS256 with a guessable secret.

stages.title: kid header chooses the key
stages.defense: Loads the HMAC key named by the token's kid header.

stages.title: RS256/HS256 confusion
stages.defense: Verifies the token with the RSA public key.

stages.title: Verified, pinned algorithm
stages.defense: Verifies HS256 with a strong server-side secret; pins alg.
